decks.notes: The deck focuses on using Balancing Act to reset the board and gain a significant advantage over the opponent, while also utilizing Burning Wish to tutor for specific cards needed for the combo. It includes various ways of generating mana quickly, such as Lion's Eye Diamond and Lotus Petal, in order to cast the key spells like Balancing Act. The deck also includes ways to draw cards and recur important spells, such as Deep Analysis and Recoup. The strategy revolves around setting up a powerful combo turn to overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate to deal with threats, while also disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Duress and Gerrard's Verdict. The deck also includes powerful finishers in Exalted Angel and Eternal Dragon to close out the game. Additionally, there are land destruction elements with cards like Wasteland and Dust Bowl to further disrupt the opponent's mana base. The overall strategy is to outlast the opponent and gain incremental advantages to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence with aggressive elves while utilizing cards like Wirewood Symbiote and Quirion Ranger to generate mana and untap creatures for repeated activations. The inclusion of Survival of the Fittest allows for tutoring key creatures like Deranged Hermit to swarm the board with tokens. The deck also includes removal in the form of Goblin Sharpshooter and Thornscape Battlemage to control the opponent's board. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient creature-based strategy.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly with Elves and utilizing cards like Gaea's Cradle and Priest of Titania to ramp up to powerful creatures like Deranged Hermit and Kamahl, Fist of Krosa. It also includes disruptive elements like Wirewood Symbiote and Thornscape Battlemage to slow down the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Quirion Ranger and Caller of the Claw provides flexibility and resilience against removal spells. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient mana production to secure a fast victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing Hermit Druid to dump the entire library into the graveyard, then using various combos like Phyrexian Devourer and Triskelion to win the game. It also has a toolbox element with cards like Karmic Guide and Palinchron for recursion and infinite mana. The deck runs disruption with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to protect the combo, while also running a slight reanimation package with Animate Dead and Krosan Reclamation. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently to win the game.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and board wipes while also limiting the opponent's resources through cards like Standstill and Dust Bowl. The goal is to maintain board presence with manlands like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave, while locking down the opponent's threats. The deck also includes card draw spells like Fact or Fiction and Impulse to ensure consistent card advantage. By slowing down the game and denying the opponent resources, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in the long run.

The deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore early in the game. It includes various ways to manipulate the graveyard and bring back key cards with Replenish. Control elements are present with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, along with enchantments like Parallax Wave for board control. The deck also has some combo potential with Opalescence and Pandemonium. The mana base is built to support the different colors needed for the spells and creatures in the deck, with utility lands like Wasteland included for disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game using cards like Pyrostatic Pillar and Cursed Scroll while setting up for a powerful combo with Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Bloodfire Colossus. The deck also includes efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Pyrokinesis to deal with threats, along with additional burn spells like Fireblast and Incinerate for direct damage. The inclusion of Gaea's Blessing helps with deck manipulation and graveyard recursion, while cards like Naturalize provide utility against artifacts and enchantments. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and overwhelm them with powerful threats.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game using counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage through cards like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush. Psychatog is the win condition of the deck, able to quickly grow in power and close out the game. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own resources, ultimately securing victory through efficient play and careful resource management.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creature cards and then bring them back from the dead using reanimation spells like Reanimate and Exhume. Creatures like Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Phantom Nishoba provide powerful threats that can swing the game in your favor once they hit the battlefield. The deck also features disruption cards like Cabal Therapy to control the opponent's resources and maintain your own game plan. This deck requires careful planning and timing to successfully execute the combo strategy and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures.

The deck focuses on efficiently dealing damage to the opponent quickly by playing cheap and aggressive creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic, while using burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate to remove blockers or finish off the opponent. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Ball Lightning and Sulfuric Vortex to close out the game. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure and keep the opponent on the back foot, while using burn spells to clear the way for your creatures to attack and secure the win.

The main focus of this deck is to use cards like Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful, game-ending combo. The deck also includes cards like Accumulated Knowledge and Portent for card draw and manipulation, as well as Counterspells and other disruptive spells to control the game. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces quickly while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a win.

This deck focuses on using Madness cards to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient spells. By discarding cards like Arrogant Wurm and Wonder to trigger Madness abilities and set up powerful plays with cards like Circular Logic and Quiet Speculation, the deck aims to push through damage quickly and efficiently. With a combination of strong creatures, removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, and card draw options like Deep Analysis, this deck is built to maintain pressure on the opponent and secure victories.

The main idea of this deck is to use the Madness mechanic to quickly and aggressively play powerful creatures like Arrogant Wurm and Basking Rootwalla. By discarding cards with Madness costs, the deck can efficiently pump out threats and overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes card draw and countermagic to maintain momentum and disrupt the opponent's plans. With a combination of aggressive creatures and versatile spells, this deck aims to apply pressure and control the game from the early turns to secure a fast victory.
